skill.name: templates
skill.description: Project scaffolding templates for new applications. Use when creating new projects from scratch. Contains 12 templates for various tech stacks.
skill.summary: Quick-start project scaffolding templates for 12 popular tech stacks including Next.js, React Native, Python FastAPI, and more.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The skill contains only markdown documentation files (TEMPLATE.md) with project scaffolding instructions. Detected patterns (backticks in code blocks, 'description' in YAML frontmatter, environment variable documentation) are documentation content, not executable code. This is a read-only reference skill with no security risks.

content.user_title: Scaffold Projects Instantly with 12 Pro Templates
content.value_statement: Stop reinventing project structure. This skill provides production-ready templates for 12 tech stacks with documented best practices, directory structures, and setup commands. Claude reads only the template matching your project type.
actual_capabilities: Provides 12 ready-to-use project templates for web, mobile, desktop, and CLI applications
Includes documented tech stacks with framework versions and recommended libraries
Specifies directory structures and file organization patterns
Lists setup commands and installation steps for each template type
Documents environment variables and configuration requirements
Offers best practices guidance for each technology stack
limitations: Templates are documentation only - Claude does not automatically create files or run commands
User must manually execute the suggested commands and create the project structure
Templates may not match every specific project requirement or preference
Limited to the 12 included tech stacks - custom frameworks not supported

use_cases.title: Rapid Prototyping
use_cases.description: Entrepreneurs and developers who need to quickly spin up new applications with proven architecture patterns.

use_cases.title: Learning New Stacks
use_cases.description: Developers learning a new framework who want to understand industry-standard project structure and best practices.
use_cases.target_user: Developers expanding their tech stack knowledge

use_cases.title: Team Standardization
use_cases.description: Teams establishing consistent project scaffolding across multiple applications using the same technology stack.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Project Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Next.js full-stack application with authentication and a PostgreSQL database.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new web application from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Stack-Specific Request
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to build a mobile app using React Native. Set up the project structure with navigation and state management.

prompt_templates.title: Comparison Inquiry
prompt_templates.prompt: What are the differences between the Next.js SaaS template and the Next.js full-stack template? Which should I use for a subscription-based application?
prompt_templates.scenario: Choosing the right template for your use case

prompt_templates.title: Advanced SaaS Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SaaS application using the Next.js SaaS template. Include Stripe payments for subscriptions, NextAuth for authentication, and Prisma for the database.

output_examples.input: Create a new Express REST API with TypeScript and JWT authentication
output_examples.output: Claude will read the express-api/TEMPLATE.md and provide: The recommended tech stack (Express + TypeScript + JWT), the complete directory structure, required packages to install, setup steps in order, and best practices for API security and error handling.

output_examples.input: I want to build a Flutter app with Riverpod for state management
output_examples.output: Claude will read the flutter-app/TEMPLATE.md and provide: The Flutter project structure, Riverpod architecture patterns, recommended dependencies, navigation setup, and state management best practices specific to Flutter.

output_examples.input: What template should I use for a documentation site?
output_examples.output: Claude will recommend the astro-static/TEMPLATE.md and explain: Why Astro is ideal for content-focused sites, the MDX content collection setup, static site generation benefits, and how to deploy to Vercel or Netlify.

best_practices: Always specify your project type clearly (e.g., 'SaaS app' vs 'static site') so Claude selects the correct template
Review the entire template before starting development to understand the recommended architecture
Follow the setup steps in order - many templates depend on previous steps being completed first
Customize the suggested structure to fit your specific requirements rather than following it blindly
anti_patterns: Asking Claude to create projects from multiple templates simultaneously - this creates conflicting architectures
Requesting features that contradict the template's design pattern (e.g., adding server-side rendering to a static site template)
Skipping the environment variable setup steps - this causes runtime errors in database-connected applications
Mixing frameworks across templates (e.g., using Next.js components in an Express API template)

faq.question: Does this skill automatically create project files for me?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides documentation and templates. You must manually run the commands and create the files that Claude suggests based on the template.

faq.question: Can I customize the templates or mix components from different templates?
faq.answer: Yes, the templates are starting points. You can adapt them to your needs, but be aware that mixing patterns from different templates may create architectural inconsistencies.

faq.question: What if my tech stack is not in the list of 12 templates?
faq.answer: The skill currently supports 12 specific stacks. If your stack is not listed, Claude will suggest the closest match or recommend creating a custom structure.

faq.question: Do these templates include production-ready code or just structure?
faq.answer: The templates provide directory structure, recommended packages, and setup guidance. They document best practices but do not include complete production code implementations.

faq.question: How often are the templates updated with new framework versions?
faq.answer: Templates are maintained by the community. Check the source repository for update history. You may need to adjust version numbers in the setup commands.

faq.question: Can I use these templates for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the templates are licensed under MIT, which permits commercial use. However, always review the licenses of the frameworks and libraries themselves.
